front: Poem: opens "'Twas mercy brought me from my Pagan land"; warns those who see "colour" as a "diabolic die" that those "black as Cain, may be refin'd"
back: "On Being Brought from Africa to America" (Phillis Wheatley)
work: On Being Brought from Africa to America

front: Poem: opens "Celestial choir! enthron'd in realms of light" and invokes Columbia; commands "Proceed, great chief, with virtue on thy side"
Poem: elegy opening "Hail, happy saint, on thine immortal throne" for a preacher who "long'd to see America excel"
back: "On the Death of the Rev. Mr. George Whitefield" (Phillis Wheatley)
work: On the Death of the Rev. Mr. George Whitefield

front: Author: Jupiter Hammon called this poet a "pious youth" in an "Address" to her; she was the first African-American woman to publish a book of poetry
Author: Robert Hayden wrote "A Letter from" this poet, in which a chimney sweep boy asks if she also sweeps chimneys
